preamble: The reduction or lenition of palatal nasals into palatal glides is a common feature in many languages, often involving the loss of nasal airflow while maintaining the palatal point of articulation.

examples.note: The Brazilian Portuguese 'nh' is often realized as a nasalized palatal glide rather than a full palatal nasal stop.
In some yeísta dialects, the palatal nasal /ɲ/ can weaken further towards a palatal glide.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
